segments.enText: It's Sukkos! Time to Unite All Jews Like One to Serve Hashem

It is important to think a bit before you do a mitzvah so that you will be doing it for the correct reasons! This is especially true with the mitzvah of lulav and esrog, since it carries with it so many different symbolisms and has deep Kabbalistic meaning. Therefore, some say the following *Yehi Ratzon* before taking the lulav.

segments.heText: יְהִי רָצוֹן מִלְּפָנֶיךָ יְהוָה אֱלֹהַי וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתַי, בִּפְרִי עֵץ הָדָר וְכַפֹּת תְּמָרִים וַעֲנַף עֵץ עָבוֹת וְעַרְבֵי נַחַל אוֹתִיּוֹת שִׁמְךָ הַמְּיֻחָד תְּקָרֵב אֶחָד אֶל אֶחָד וְהָיוּ לַאֲחָדִים בְּיָדִי וְלֵידַע אֵיךְ שִׁמְךָ נִקְרָא עָלַי וְיִירְאוּם מִגֶּשֶׁת אֵלִי, וּבְנַעֲנוּעַי אוֹתָם תַּשְׁפִּיעַ שֶׁפַע בְּרָכוֹת מֵדַעַת עֶלְיוֹן לִוְנֶה אֶפְרִיוֹן לִמְכוֹן בֵּית אֱלֹהֵינוּ, וּתְהֵא חֲשׁוּבָה לְפָנֶיךָ מִצְוַת אַרְבָּעָה מִינִים אֵלּוּ, כְּאִלּוּ קִיַּמְתִּיהָ בְּכָל פְּרָטוֹתֶיהָ וְשָׁרָשֶׁיהָ וְתַרְיַ"ג מִצְוֹת הַתְּלוּיוֹת בָּהּ: כִּי כַוָּנָתִי לְיַחֵד גְּ"דְ שְׁמָא דְקֻדְשָׁא בְּרִיךְ הוּא וּשְׁכִינְתֵּיהּ בִּדְחִילוּ וּרְחִימוּ לְיַחֵד שֵׁם י"ה בְּו"ה בְּיִחוּדָא שְׁלִים בְּשֵׁם כָּל יִשְׂרָאֵל אָמֵן: בָּרוּךְ יְהוָה לְעוֹלָם אָמֵן וְאָמֵן:

segments.enText: **יְהִי רָצוֹן מִלְּפָנֶיךָ**—May it be Your will, Hashem, that by me taking the Arba'ah Minim—the esrog, lulav, hadassim, and aravos (*which, according to the Kabbalah, correspond to the four letters of the Name of Hashem*)—the four letters of Your Name "become united in my hand"—that I will feel Your Divine Presence, and that any evil will sense Your Presence and therefore be afraid to attack me. And may my waving of the Arba'ah Minim cause blessing to flow down from the Heavens down to the place where Your Beis Hamikdash (*which will hopefully soon be rebuilt*) resides. and (*even though I know that I do not always do all the mitzvos perfectly and with the proper intentions*) may Hashem consider this mitzvah as if I did it 100 percent properly!

segments.heText: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, אֲשֶׁר קִדְּשָׁנוּ בְּמִצְוֹתָיו, וְצִוָּנוּ עַל נְטִילַת לוּלָב.

segments.enText: **בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה**—You, Hashem, are the Source of everything; **אֱלֹהֵינוּ**—You take care of us; **הָעוֹלָם**—and You rule the entire world; **אֲשֶׁר קִדְּשָׁנוּ בְּמִצְוֹתָיו**—and through Your mitzvos You elevated us; **עַל נְטִילַת לוּלָב**—and commanded us concerning the mitzvah of taking the lulav.
If this is the first time doing the mitzvah of the Arba'ah Minim this year, say the following to thank Hashem for this opportunity:
segments.condition: first time doing the mitzvah of the Arba'ah Minim this year

segments.heText: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, שֶׁהֶחֱיָנוּ וְקִיְּמָנוּ וְהִגִּיעָנוּ לַזְּמַן הַזֶּה:
segments.condition: first time doing the mitzvah of the Arba'ah Minim this year

segments.enText: **בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה**—You, Hashem, are the Source of everything; **אֱלֹהֵינוּ**—You take care of us; **הָעוֹלָם**—and You rule the entire world; **שֶׁהֶחֱיָנוּ וְקִיְּמָנוּ וְהִגִּיעָנוּ לַזְּמַן**—Who has granted us life, sustained us, and enabled us to reach this occasion.
segments.condition: first time doing the mitzvah of the Arba'ah Minim this year
